sentences.text: Hey, friendly!
Good morning!
Good morning!
How are you?
Good!
Hold on, let me see about this camera.
I have two… so I think this one will….
Hi!
So good to meet you guys, how are you?
It's you too!
Doing well.
How about you?
Good!
My husband and I recently moved to Raleigh, North Carolina, and we were living in Miami for, like, almost 5 years total.
So it's just been so peaceful these last couple of weeks, like.
Not having to hear a ton of… like, honking all the time, and, like, everything is 15 to 20 minutes away.
Yeah.
Like, yeah, the Whole Foods is 15-20 minutes away, the mall, like, even going up to Durham.
from here is 30 minutes, like, on the dot.
So, it's been… and I haven't really experienced a lot of traffic around here lately, so that's been super nice.
… he went… he graduated from Duke, like, decades ago now, but, … it's been cool to visit campus as, like, the kids are moving in, and him just, like, re-experiencing all of that.
So, yeah, I've had myself a little time.
I love that.
What brought you here?
We're both in Wisconsin, in, like, Madison area.
Oh, so you guys are like, okay, got it, got it.
What brought us here?

We move around a ton, so, prior to this role, I was on site, and I was doing, all of the lease-ups for… so I started out with Pizzuto, and then I went to Alliance Residential, through, like, the acquisition with Graystar.
Yeah.

And with Alliance, I did… lease up, so we did our flagship property in New Brunswick, which is where I met my husband, and then with him, we moved from New Brunswick to Maryland for a bit, then from Maryland, we went to Yonkers, did the lease up there, and then from Yonkers, New York, we went to Hingham, Massachusetts, so just south of Boston.
So we were there for a little bit, and then after Graystar took over, they were like, oh, well, we don't really have, like, a lease-up position, so do you just want to stay?
And it was at… just at that moment where Boston was getting cold in like, July, August, so we were like, okay, no, no, we're good, we'll move to Florida.
And so we just moved to Florida, and then I did, … a little bit more about my background.

That is when I went into sort of, like, a sales enablement, role with two different startups, and then from there, I took, a learning and development role with RPM, so I was there for about 2-3 years, and then from there, I really wanted to Kind of fine-tune some more of, like.

the marketing pieces, because it just, like, supported the on-site team so much, and it was so hand-in-hand with, like, L&D, the way we ran it at, RPM, so… Then, I took a role with Advineer, which is, like, a really small company, and then I've just realized I really enjoy, like, coaching, kind of… sales, like, tips, like, best practices and creating, like, optimizing processes to lead to, like, KPIs and stuff, so I took a role at Rent as the Senior District Sales Manager, for Florida, and then… rent, obviously, with the Zillow partnership, kind of, like.

just, like, went away, and so, then I was like, well, I really enjoyed my marketing role, and, so I kind of just went back there, and honestly, rent was super cool because there were so many different products, like the social product, like the AI product, like DMS, and just ILS, and… I learned so much more.
… that, like, helps me in this marketing role.
So even from Advenir, skip a year to now, like, I've just personally grown so much, so….
Yeah, that's awesome.
Oh, I feel like, why North Carolina?
Sorry.
No!
mal.
Oh, no, no!
That's your question.
Oh, why not?

North Carolina, just… well, just because we're used to moving so much, and, yeah, we kind of got… we're like, alright, I think our Florida chapter's done, and our family's on Long Island, and, we were like, okay, well, let's… I think we have one more city in us, and so we were like, yeah, like, let's do it, like, Raleigh, North Carolina, close enough to Duke, and, like, that halfway point between Florida and, like, making it all the way Home to… to family, so… That is why.
We've got Ben all over.
like a TLDR, like TLDR, we move a lot.
Well, it's nice that you have the ability to move a lot as well.
And it sounds like you've seen a lot of cool places, too, so….
Yeah, yeah, it's been fun.
Cool places, cool workplaces, yeah, I've had a blast.
Cool.

Well, yeah, I know a little bit about Rentable, … I know, I feel like there's, like, Mavin, like, there's different pieces, like the… the AI tool that, like, scrapes, kind of for occupancy, but then there's a piece that manages the Google Business profile, like, postings and updates and stuff, so… yeah, I'm here for whatever, and then obviously, specifically, Palm Breeze is, one of the… is why this conversation got started.

Yep, yeah, I obviously got that email, and I thought it would just make more sense to chat through it, because there are some different pieces of this, and it wasn't… the contract that this property on wasn't just straight for the rentable ILS.

So, we have been working with Mel and Taylor and the team on the Maven AI side for the Google Business Automation, and then we did… we did a little deep dive into Palm Breeze to figure out that agreement, and it looks like that agreement was started with Weller.
And that contract is kind of a combination.
So it does have, like, one of the basic pieces of our Maven AI product.
Before it became Maven, it was called Google Business Pro, and it was really, like, daily posts.
So, there's daily posts on the Google profile for that property, and then we also have the rentable ILS.
So I'm not familiar if you're… I don't know if you're familiar with the rentable ILS, but I could walk you through what that entails if you wanted.
Yeah, let's do it.
Yeah.
So, this product is… was really built because a couple years back, we kept hearing from customers that their marketing plans were kind of seasonal.

So, companies were wanting to really spend a lot of money with marketing and ILSs during their high seasons, and then they would cancel it or tailor it down when they didn't have as many units to rent.
So, we came up with kind of a marketing plan that is a lower cost month over month.
To get you, consistent exposure across multiple platforms.

So, we have the Rentable ILS, you have a listing for Palm Breeze on Rentable, but then we are also syndicating out to a large amount of partner sites to get you exposure across a huge network, and then we also, for this property, roped in, some spend on social.
So, it's kind of an all-encompassing way to get consistent exposure across multiple platforms, ours, and then getting some targeted ads on Instagram and Facebook.
Morgan, anything I'm missing there?
No, nothing… nothing there.
The… the contract?
It was signed quite a while ago.
It was a 2022 agreement, so it has been running since then.
You've been getting exposure and leads and posts every single day on Google since then.
But yeah, it's definitely, like, a little bit more outdated compared to what we're… Providing now, contract-wise and product-wise, so….
Yep.
For this property specifically, Nicole, are you guys focusing on ILSs?
Is this property needing of a large amount of leases, or how is the property sitting right now?
Yeah, so this is a really unique property right now because, so they're all townhomes, …
They are in need of Leases, but it's… It's so, …
the client… so here's what happened.
We were leasing, and we were doing great, and then the client decided to essentially, like, not renew a lot of people, so that he can sell the townhomes individually.
And so, the residents were aware that that's what was happening, that we were non-renewing, people were moving out, and then we were listing the individual townhomes for sale.
Not we, the client.
Sure.
The client then found out, hey, it's actually not that easy and quick to sell homes, so he wanted then for us to put, like, all gears on for leasing homes.
However, let's say he had 15 empty townhomes.
he didn't want us to list all 15 as available.
He only wants hand-selected units to be listed.
The reason is, is because he doesn't want someone who might be interested in purchasing, a townhome to go on the website and see, oh, wait, but you're also leasing it?
Question mark.
So… That's what's happening at this community.
Okay.
Yeah.
Additionally, there is an HOA side to Palm Breezes, We don't have anything to do with them.
… So, yes, we do need to lease, but there's not really a specific target goal, … Which makes this community, like, pretty difficult to… market, ….
Yeah.
Yeah, that's where we are with this community.
Go.

Yeah, and I know with conversations with the marketing team, with Mel, she's been saying that you guys have been focusing a lot more on the Google Business profile side of it, and really putting more money into SEO and organic traffic to the profiles.
So I… I would love to show you that product and see what your thoughts are on… because you really have, like, the base package for… for this property.
Let me show you… let's see…
Let me show you a profile that's fully built out.
So, what you have for Palm Breeze right now is the daily posts.
So, this product is built to completely automate the process of posting over the profiles, it's built to fully optimize and fill out your profile, to feed Google what it wants.
So, when… prospects are searching in your area for apartments, your profile's gonna be ranked higher because of the amount that we're pushing and optimizing into the profile.
So for problem reasons right now, we're not using the profile manager.
You just have these daily posts running.
So, because this contract was signed a couple years ago, we had, like, the base version of this, where it was just the posts.
So, for Palm Bres, you have the daily posts that are running like this.
Yeah.
but not the rest of the product.
And the rest of the product entails posting, live pricing and availability, all directly from your feed, so there's no work that needs to be done on your end other than turning the feed on.
But this… the rest of the features include pulling over pricing and availability, This is updated every day.
To reflect what your property has available now.

So, if leasing is something you guys are needing, and needing to fill vacant units, this is also a good way to do that, because it shows the prospect what you have available right now, and then this reflects the lowest price per this unit type.
Okay.
We can also pull in… amenities.
So, we're pulling in amenities directly from the feed to the profile to also give a visual to prospects of what this property has, both in-unit and community level.
And then the last piece is the Q&A.
Do you know if you guys are doing anything with Q&A for this property?
No, I don't think so, we're not.
So, this is all….
Oh, it looks like there is some….
Here, I can also just open that property, too.
It looks like we do have….
Yeah, so this is the posting that we're doing.
So, just pulling the photos and the amenity-based information from the feed to the profile.
And then we are displaying concessions as well.
So this is pulling live concession data from the feed to the profile.
But yeah, then if we added the other features, you would get the pricing and availability, and then the Q&A.

Q&A's been a big focus for our customers this year, because of AI overviews, and how… how… that content is being read, so we know that Google really likes the setup of, like, FAQs, and we've had a lot of customers focusing on FAQs on websites.
This is the same thing, just getting it live on your Google profile, too.
Okay, cool.
Yeah, we did.
We did.
We have, like, FAQs for almost every one of our websites.
Okay, cool.
So, okay, so to palm breezes right now, right now, we're paying, what is it?
How much is the monthly contract right now?
Because one of our VPs said, and again, this is, like, how this conversation came up, she had said, hey.
Is rentable something we can cut?
Because I have an invoice for $400.
And I was like, I don't… is it $400 a month, or is maybe there… there was unpaid invoices, and… yeah.
I can answer that.
Yes, so… The contract as it was signed then had… it was the featured listing for $100 a month.
It was a targeted social boost that Kylie mentioned, where we're also able to put some ad spend on Facebook and Instagram.
That was $150 a month add-on, and then the Google business Automation for those postings were also $150 a month, so that $400, that's kind of where that breakdown came from.

But kind of how Kylie mentioned, like, now we have… we have, like, a new pricing structure with Brighton Properties that are using this Google tool, and we also have different pricing packages for the listings and the ad side of things, so… Going forward, we can definitely, like, re-contract this to fit what you guys are needing now.
Price-wise and product-wise.

So, it's definitely… because this contract is currently just rolling month-to-month, so it would be easy to cut it if that's what you wanted to do, or it'd be really easy to re-contract it and just, … do just, like, the Google as a separate contract and align it with what we're doing with the rest of the properties current day, and if you still wanted some lead exposure, we can get you a different package there, too.
Yeah, I'm open to you… Kylie, thank you so much, and Morgan, thank you.
I'm open to your recommendations for… for this one, since it is month-to-month.
I don't necessarily… want to say, like, oh, yeah, let's just, like, cancel everything, because I do really enjoy the profile manager.
Love the daily posts, ….
And honestly, like.
if there's a way to… I'm not that….
Like, wild about the listing, because again.
why spend the money on driving traffic to, like, a listing when there's only such limited, like, availability that we're updating every once in a while?
So I would rather put…
spend less than $400, but add on, like, that live pricing and availability that gets updated daily, again, based on whatever we may adjust.
Continue the… the posts and… yeah, if we can keep it under 100 for… or 400 for the Q&A section, then, like, great.
So if you guys have a recommendation for that, Open to seeing that, and then, yeah, re-contracting for that.
My recommendation for the Google side with Maven would be to re-contract it under what Brighton has been re-signing.
So, we gave, like, a bulk price because they'd rolled out a good amount of properties for $99 a property.
So, that would get you locked in to that lower price, for the Google side of it, and then we'd get you those additional features as well.
Okay.

My recommendation for that would be… we're seeing, like, a 20% increase in traffic for the properties that we've added with Brighton, and that would be from the time they rolled us out compared to the same time period last year.

So, we are seeing a good amount of traffic to those, so you're still going to be getting, you know, calls, but then those prospects will be able to see what's available right now with pricing and availability.
So that would definitely be my recommendation on the Google side.
Pop.
Cool.
Advertising-wise.
I mean, we would be open… I can loop in someone from the advertising side, too, to give me updated pricing, but we're open to kind of what you're thinking.
If you would rather just cut the advertising spend, like you said, with the limited availability, that's fine, since you're month-to-month, I think that's totally doable.

Otherwise, if you wanted to just go with… a similar package, where you have a featured listing in the social ad spend, but for less than what you're currently paying, I could also see if that's an option, too.
Okay, cool, yeah, if you can see if that second one's an option, that'd be great.
And then if you can just email me, like, the different, pricings, what it includes, The client always loves a good year-over-year 20% increase, so… Yeah, let's type that in there.
And then, yeah, this client is super, super hands-on.
I mean, you probably hear that a lot, but….
This one's a little much.
Like, he wants to see income verifications himself, like, it's so crazy, … And I'm like, bro, like, your product isn't even that… like, can you relax, sir, please?
Yeah.
I literally just want to, like, like a child, like, put your arm out to his forehead and be like, hold on, stop kicking and screaming, please.
No, that'd be great, that'd be super helpful, thank you so much if you guys can do that.
… Yeah, sometime this week.
Yeah.
Is there anything else that you wanted to go over with me?
Does… do you have any other properties with that ownership group?
No.
Okay.
Any other properties that you'd be interested in the Maven, the Google tool for?
Yes, I think so.
Let me go through my portfolio, and let you know, because I haven't, I haven't gone through it.

Honestly, I have, … This week, I have the chunk of my monthly marketing calls with my asset directors and community managers, so as I go through prepping my parts for those meetings, I'll go through and start scraping every, profile page, see Where they are, occupancy-wise, and then get back to you on, … Where I think it may be, helpful, too.
Okay.
Well, we'll, put together the pricing that we just talked about, we'll get that in front of you, written out.

I'll send over… I'll send over… I have a one-sheeter that I made for Brighton for the Google tool, so you have that, and then I'll give you a snapshot of the analytics so you have that for this customer.
Perfect.
That sounds great.
Let us know if there's anything else we can provide as well.
I will, thank you guys so much, I appreciate your time!
Yes, of course.
And for listening!
Into my, like, really long rants about movies.
Of course!
I love it.
I was like, why am I talking about this?
It's making me kind of want to move now.
North Carolina's a beautiful place, so far.
It's only been a couple weeks.
We'll see about the winter.
Alright, you guys, well, have a good rest of your day, and I appreciate it.
Basically.
Sue, thank you.
Bye.
